children.name: Trachyphonus
children.description: Large, strikingly patterned African barbets with bold spotted and barred plumage and a distinctive crest. They are more terrestrial than other barbets, often foraging on the ground for insects and fallen fruit. They are highly social and nest in burrows excavated in banks or termite mounds.

children.name: Trachyphonus vaillantii
children.description: A familiar and widespread barbet of southern and East African savannas and woodlands. Its plumage is a striking combination of black, white, yellow, and red, with a spiky black crest. Its loud, trilling call is a characteristic sound of African gardens and bushveld.

children.name: Trachyphonus erythrocephalus
children.description: A brilliantly coloured barbet of East Africa, from Ethiopia and Somalia south to Tanzania. The male has a vivid red head and breast, a yellow belly, and black and white spotted upperparts. It inhabits dry savanna and scrub, nesting in holes excavated in termite mounds or riverbanks.

children.name: Pogoniulus
children.description: Tiny barbets known as tinkerbirds, named for their repetitive, bell-like calls that resemble a blacksmith's hammer on an anvil. They are among the smallest of all barbets, with a plump, compact body and a short bill. Despite their size, their calls can carry for long distances.

children.name: Pogoniulus chrysoconus
children.description: A widespread tinkerbird found across sub-Saharan Africa in savanna and woodland habitats. It has a distinctive yellow forehead patch, black and white striped head, and a yellow rump. Its monotonous, penetrating call — a series of identical 'tink' notes — is one of the most familiar bird sounds of the African bush.

children.name: Lybius
children.description: Medium-sized barbets with a robust bill and colourful head and neck patterns. They are typically found in savanna and scrub habitats across sub-Saharan Africa, feeding heavily on figs and other fruits. They nest in tree cavities excavated with their powerful bills.

children.name: Lybius torquatus
children.description: A common and widespread barbet of southern and East Africa, from South Africa to Kenya. It has a red head and breast, a grey-brown back, and a distinctive black collar across the upper breast. Its loud, duetting call is a familiar sound in gardens and woodlands, often performed as a coordinated antiphonal duet between mates.

children.name: Lybius leucocephalus
children.description: Found across Central and East Africa from Nigeria to Tanzania, inhabiting savanna, woodland, and forest edges. It has a white head, throat, and breast contrasting with a black back, wings, and tail. It is often seen in pairs or small family groups, feeding on figs and other soft fruit.
